metadata.title: BreakHeroes — Travel at the right time
metadata.description: Find the best time to travel — not just the cheapest. We analyze holidays, crowds, and seasonality to tell you exactly when to go, and when to avoid.
metadata.ogDescription: Timing intelligence for travelers. Find the best windows, avoid the worst.

footer.tagline: Travel timing intelligence. Pick the right window, not just the cheapest flight.
footer.disclaimer: Recommendations are directional. Prices and demand vary.

hero.badge: New — timing intelligence, not another booking site
hero.title: Find the <highlight>best time</highlight> to travel — not just the cheapest.
hero.subtitle: We analyze public holidays, school breaks, crowds, and seasonality to tell you exactly when to go — and when to avoid.

howItWorks.title: Three signals, one clear answer
howItWorks.subtitle: We combine the forces that actually shape your trip — not just flight prices.

originPressure.title: Origin pressure
originPressure.description: When your home country's school breaks line up with your dates, demand (and prices) surge on outbound flights.

destinationDisruption.title: Destination disruption
destinationDisruption.description: Local holidays close attractions, pack cities, or move prices at your destination independent of your origin.

seasonality.title: Seasonality
seasonality.description: Weather, daylight, and natural events decide whether the dates are actually pleasant — or miserable.

exampleResult.title: Example: Japan in spring
exampleResult.subtitle: The same trip can cost 2× or feel like a different country depending on the week. Here's what timing intelligence looks like for a family traveling from the UK.

about.metaDescription: The methodology behind our travel timing recommendations — origin pressure, destination disruption, and seasonality.
about.title: How we pick the best time
about.intro: Most travel sites optimize for flights. We optimize for whether the trip is actually going to be good.

how.heading: Three signals, one score
how.p1: Every candidate date window gets scored on three independent layers. We weight them based on your priority (cheapest, least-crowded, best weather, or balanced), then combine them into a final score from 0 to 100.

originPressure.heading: 1. Origin pressure
originPressure.body: Demand from your home country. When UK school half-term lines up with your dates, outbound flights from London spike — even if your destination is quiet. We track school calendars and major holidays for your origin market to measure this pressure.

destinationDisruption.heading: 2. Destination disruption
destinationDisruption.body: Local forces at your destination — Golden Week in Japan, Ferragosto in Italy, Tết in Vietnam. These change what's open, how crowded cities are, and whether the trip actually works. Origin pressure and destination disruption are different signals and we model them separately.

seasonality.heading: 3. Seasonality
seasonality.body: Weather, daylight, natural events. Cherry blossoms peak at a specific week, typhoon season is real, and shoulder months often pair great weather with low demand. We encode each destination's best, acceptable, and poor months, plus weather modifiers like rainy and typhoon seasons.

data.heading: Where the data comes from
data.p1: Public holiday data is computed from government-sourced rule sets — fixed dates, Easter calculations, lunar-calendar holidays, and weekend-shift rules. School holiday data is curated from education ministry publications.
data.p2: All data lives in the repository. Nothing is fetched at runtime. This keeps the site fast, cheap, and available even if upstream APIs go down.

prices.label: Real-time prices.
prices.body: We don't scrape flights or hotels. Our "cheaper" signal is based on seasonal and holiday-adjacent patterns, not live inventory.

bookings.label: Bookings.
bookings.body: We're a timing layer, not a reseller. When you find your window, book through the provider of your choice.

guarantees.label: Guarantees.
guarantees.body: Our recommendations are directional. Weather varies, events get rescheduled, and prices can surprise anyone.

q1.q: Why monthly windows instead of exact dates?
q1.a: Most travelers have a few weeks of flexibility, not a specific date. Monthly windows let us give you clear, comparable signals. We'll refine to week-level windows as data improves.

q2.q: Will you add more origin countries?
q2.a: Yes — the MVP ships with UK. We'll expand to other major markets based on demand.

q3.q: Can I trust this for a big trip?
q3.a: Use it as one input. We're giving you signal the big booking sites hide — but the final call should factor your constraints, flexibility, and appetite for risk.
